title: SequentialBreak: Large Language Models Can be Fooled by Embedding Jailbreak Prompts into Sequential Prompt Chains
collaborators: Md. Rafi Ur Rashid, PhD Student, Pennsylvania State University, USA
description: As large language models move into real products, their safety guardrails stay surprisingly brittle. SequentialBreak exposes a striking blind spot: burying a harmful request inside a chain of ordinary ones slips it past these defenses in a single query, revealing how fragile the alignment really is.

title: The Art of Saying “Maybe”: A Conformal Lens for Uncertainty Benchmarking in VLMs
collaborators: Dr. Mohammad Saifur Rahman, Professor, BUET
Md. Rizwan Parvez, Research Scientist, QCRI
description: Today's vision-language models stay confident even when they are wrong. This work tackles a neglected question, do they know what they don't know, delivering the first broad benchmark of their uncertainty and a way to measure it even for closed, commercial models.

title: Harf-Speech: A Clinically Aligned Framework for Arabic Phoneme-Level Speech Assessment
collaborators: Dr. Ehsan Hoque, Professor, University of Rochester (MIT PhD)
affiliation: Remote AI Research Engineer, Ministry of Defense Healthcare Services, Saudi Arabia
description: What if speech assessment were available anytime, anywhere, without losing clinical quality? With Arabic-trained speech pathologists scarce, Harf-Speech automatically evaluates Arabic (MSA) at the same fine-grained level clinicians use, with results validated against certified specialists.

title: Digital Harf: A Clinically Integrated Multimodal AI System for Pervasive Arabic Speech and Language Therapy
collaborators: Dr. Ehsan Hoque, Professor, University of Rochester (MIT PhD)
affiliation: Remote AI Research Engineer, Ministry of Defense Healthcare Services, Saudi Arabia
description: Children with autism in Arabic-speaking regions face a shortage of specialists and almost no culturally relevant therapy material. Digital Harf carries clinician-led speech therapy into the home through an AI engine that generates culturally grounded content, treating cultural fit as core infrastructure rather than an afterthought.

title: Robustness of Vision Language Models Against Split-Image Harmful Input Attacks
collaborators: Dr. Shagufta Mehnaz, Assistant Professor, Pennsylvania State University, USA
Md. Rafi Ur Rashid, PhD Student, Pennsylvania State University, USA
Vishnu Dasu, PhD Student, Pennsylvania State University, USA
description: Vision-language models have learned to refuse harmful images, but only when the harm arrives in one piece. This work reveals that splitting a harmful image across fragments quietly defeats their safety training, introduces attacks that expose this overlooked gap, and proposes a defense to close it.

title: DFCon: Attention-Driven Supervised Contrastive Learning for Robust Deepfake Detection
venue: IEEE Signal Processing Cup at ICASSP 2025
collaborators: Dr. Mohammad Saifur Rahman, Professor, BUET
description: As AI-generated faces grow indistinguishable from real ones, catching them in the wild remains an open problem. Leading this BUET team at IEEE ICASSP 2025, we built a deepfake detector that blends complementary vision models to generalize across unseen generation methods and source datasets.

title: AttMetNet: Attention-Enhanced Deep Neural Network for Methane Plume Detection in Sentinel-2 Satellite Imagery
collaborators: Sultanul Arifin, Lecturer, BRAC University, Dhaka
description: Methane is a potent driver of climate change, yet spotting its plumes from satellites is buried under false alarms that demand expert review. AttMetNet is a lightweight, attention-driven model that hones in on a methane-sensitive spectral signature to flag true plumes, making reliable global-scale monitoring practical.
